title: Spicy Korean Tofu Bowl
description: A flavorful and spicy Korean-inspired dish made with crispy tofu, crunchy vegetables, and a savory sauce, served in a steaming hot bowl.
instructions: 1. Cut the tofu into bite-sized cubes and coat with a mixture of 1 tsp soy sauce, 1 tsp brown sugar, and 1 tsp rice vinegar. Let marinate for at least 15 minutes.
2. In a large skillet or wok, heat 1 tbsp vegetable oil over medium-high heat.
3. Add the tofu to the skillet and cook until golden brown on all sides, about 5-7 minutes.
4. Add the sliced carrots and cucumber to the skillet and cook until they start to soften, about 3-4 minutes.
5. In a small bowl, whisk together 1 tbsp soy sauce, 2 tsp Korean chili flakes, and 2 tbsp water.
6. Pour the sauce over the vegetables and tofu, and stir-fry until the sauce has thickened and the vegetables are tender.
7. Stir in chopped green onions and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes.
8. Serve the spicy tofu mixture over a bed of cooked white rice in a steaming hot bowl.
9. Garnish with additional green onions and a sprinkle of Korean chili flakes, if desired.
